prevalence.source: ICMR/NMHS + IHME GBD (bipolar disorder) with subtype estimates

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11: at least one manic episode

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low to moderate due to treatment gaps and limited follow-up

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Diagnosis often in tertiary psychiatry; delays common in rural areas
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Mood stabilizers and antipsychotics; inpatient care for acute mania; limited psychoeducation availability
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Stigma, specialist shortage, and follow-up/monitoring barriers
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Availability of affordable generic mood stabilizers and expanding tertiary services
